stories.title: CPSC Recall — Bellabu Bear Children's Robes (Burn Hazard)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: CPSC RECALL: Bellabu Bear kids' sherpa robes (PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Minions). Burn hazard — fails flammability standards. Sold 2024-2025 for ~$60. Stop use now. Refund: 888-703-7752 or help@bellabubear.com 🔴 #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Bellabu Bear recall: sherpa kids' robes (PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Garfield, Batman) sold 2024-2025 at Saks and online. Burn hazard — fails flammability standards. Check yours. Full refund: help@bellabubear.com #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: CPSC Recall: Stop Using Bellabu Bear Children's Sherpa Robes — Burn Hazard

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Bellabu Bear Children's Robe Recall

**Image Concept 1A — Social (1200x675)**
- **Type:** gemini base_only
- **Prompt:** A clean product safety concept on a warm white background. A soft children's bathrobe in a sherpa texture, folded neatly, with a small red warning symbol or caution triangle beside it. Soft, warm lighting. No text, no logos, no specific character designs. Clean visual space in the bottom third for text overlay. Muted but clear color palette — cream, red accent. Photorealistic style.
- **Mood:** Alert, urgent but calm — parents should stop and read

**Image Concept 1B — Article Hero (1200x630)**
- **Type:** gemini base_only
- **Prompt:** Overhead view of a soft children's robe laid flat on a clean white surface, with a small red caution card placed beside it. The robe is cream or light grey in color, sherpa texture visible. Clean, clinical composition suggesting a safety inspection. No text overlays. Warm studio lighting. Photorealistic. Bottom third of image is clear for text overlay.
- **Mood:** Safety-focused, clear, trustworthy

---

research.facts:
Bellabu Bear Children's Robe Recall — Burn Hazard
**Sources:** CPSC.gov official recall page, bellabubear.com recall page, MyParisTexas, Sparkler/various media
**Verified Facts:**
- Product: Bellabu Bear bamboo sherpa children's robes [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Hazard: Violates mandatory federal flammability standards for children's sleepwear; burn hazard [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Licensed character prints: PAW Patrol, Garfield, Minecraft, Minions, Batman, Justice League, Wonder Woman, Blaze and the Monster Machines, Trolls, Dora, Nutcracker, hearts, Santa, stripes, bows, dragons [HIGH — CPSC.gov + Bellabu Bear recall page]
- Sizes: S/M and M/L [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Where sold: Saks Fifth Avenue stores and boutiques nationwide; online at bellabubear.com and other online retailers [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Sale dates: January 2024 through July 2025 [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Price: about $60 [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Incidents/injuries: None reported [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Consumer action: Immediately stop use, take away from children, contact Bellabu Bear for full refund or store credit [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Phone: 888-703-7752, 9 AM-4 PM PT, Monday-Friday [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Email: help@bellabubear.com [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
- Website: bellabubear.com [HIGH — CPSC.gov]
**Story History Check:** NEW STORY — No previous Bellabu Bear recall coverage in story history.

research.facts:
Colorado Springs 2026 Summer Camp Guide
**Sources:** coloradospringskids.com, coloradospringssummercamps.com, cospringsmom.com, Macaroni KID Colorado Springs
**Verified Facts:**
- D11 last day of school: May 22, 2026 [HIGH — schoolcalendarinfo.com + D11 official confirmed earlier in search]
- Colorado Tinkering School: STEAM day camps July 6-10, July 20-24, August 3-7; approximately 9 AM-4:30 PM [MEDIUM — search result summary]
- Go West Camps: outdoor day camp; ages 5-14; home bases at Eagleview Middle School, Chinook Trail Middle School, The Colorado Springs School [MEDIUM — search result]
- CYT Colorado Springs: theater camps; ages 5-13; payment plans available (contact Kyla Kay) [MEDIUM — search result]
- Catamount Institute: day camps ages 5-12; June and July sessions; scholarship applications available [MEDIUM — search result]
- It's FUNdamental Multisport: ages 3-11; M-Thu 9-11:30 AM; $120 per child; 25% discount for additional siblings [MEDIUM — search result summary]
- Resource: coloradosprings.macaronikid.com has 2026 Summer Camp Guide Directory [MEDIUM — search result]
- Resource: coloradospringssummercamps.com [HIGH — search result URL]

stories.title: Outdoor Play Is the Best Summer Investment — Research Roundup for COS Families

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Kids who play outside regularly show lower obesity risk, stronger bones, less stress, and better focus. Research backs unstructured outdoor time this summer. COS families: we've got the trails. 🌲 #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: Why Outdoor Play Is the Best Summer Investment — Research for COS Families

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Outdoor Play Benefits

**Image Concept 5A — Social (1200x675)**
- **Type:** gemini base_only
- **Prompt:** Two children (approximately ages 7-10) playing outdoors in a Colorado mountain setting — climbing on large boulders near a pine forest trail with Pikes Peak visible in the background on a clear sunny day. Bright, natural daylight. Warm greens, blues, earth tones. No close-up faces. Joyful, energetic posture. Clean lower third of image for text overlay. Painterly illustration style with photorealistic details.
- **Mood:** Joyful, healthy, freedom, Colorado outdoors

**Image Concept 5B — Article Hero (1200x630)**
- **Type:** gemini base_only
- **Prompt:** A wide shot of a child running through an open meadow in a Colorado mountain park setting. Pine trees frame the sides, Pikes Peak visible in the distance, clear blue sky above. The child is silhouetted or small in the frame, emphasizing the scale of the landscape. Golden hour light, warm and inviting. No text. Clean sky in upper third for text overlay. Photorealistic landscape photography style.
- **Mood:** Expansive, healthy, freedom, Colorado spirit

research.facts:
Outdoor Play Benefits — Research Roundup
**Sources:** AAP (HealthyChildren.org), CHOP (Children's Hospital of Philadelphia), UCLA Health, Sanford Health, Sparkler/Playsparkler.org, PMC research articles
**Verified Facts:**
- AAP recommends outdoor play as essential for child development [HIGH — HealthyChildren.org]
- Recommended outdoor play: approximately 3 hours/day for school-aged children [MEDIUM — Sanford Health; AAP does not cite a specific hourly figure in most guidance]
- Benefits: lower obesity risk, stronger bones/muscles [HIGH — CHOP, UCLA Health, multiple sources]
- Sun exposure builds vitamin D, supports bone health, may reduce chronic disease risk [HIGH — Sanford Health, CHOP]
- Stress reduction: stress levels drop within minutes of being outside [MEDIUM — multiple pediatric sources]
- ADHD: outdoor time reduces symptoms [MEDIUM — cited by Sanford Health referencing studies; not an AAP official statement]
- Cognitive benefits: improved focus, higher test scores in schools with environmental education programs [MEDIUM — Sanford Health]
- Social development: sharing, taking turns, socialization through unstructured outdoor play [HIGH — multiple pediatric sources]
- Colorado Springs context: access to trails, parks, and Pikes Peak open spaces [HIGH — local knowledge, not a study claim]
**Story History Check:** NEW STORY — Outdoor play/summer health angle not previously covered.
